headline: Germany seeks doubling of AI data centres by 2030
summary: Germany plans to encourage investments in data centres to at least double ‌domestic capacity and to boost artificial intelligence data ‌processing at least fourfold by 2030, the government said on Tuesday. In ​a bid to catch up with the dominant players the United States and China, digital minister Karsten Wildberger proposed a range of measures, including dedicating land for development, ‌that ministers are due ⁠to approve on Wednesday. ($1 = 0.8640 euros)

headline: Google in talks with China's Envicool, others to buy data centre cooling systems, sources say
summary: Alphabet's Google is in talks with Envicool and other Chinese firms about buying liquid cooling equipment for data centres, according to people with knowledge of a visit to China by one ‌of the U.S. firm's procurement teams.  Liquid cooling systems - which circulate water or other liquid over or around equipment - ‌have become critical in AI data centers as the high-density computing generates more heat than a traditional air cooling system can handle.  The visit by the ​team from Google's Taiwan operations this month reflects tight supply of parts for such systems, the sources said.

headline: Amazon, Google-Backed Anthropic's Rise A 'Wake-Up Call' For OpenAI, Says Executive: What The ChatGPT-Parent Is Reportedly Planning To Do About It
summary: OpenAI is reportedly pivoting toward coding and enterprise AI, scaling back its broad approach as rising competition from Anthropic and internal challenges prompt a strategic reset.

raw.headline: Amazon, Google-Backed Anthropic's Rise A 'Wake-Up Call' For OpenAI, Says Executive: What The ChatGPT-Parent Is Reportedly Planning To Do About It
raw.summary: OpenAI is reportedly pivoting toward coding and enterprise AI, scaling back its broad approach as rising competition from Anthropic and internal challenges prompt a strategic reset.
